executive_summary: Build workflow dispatch failed with HTTP 404 error. The GitHub Actions workflow file (.github/workflows/build.yml) is not found or not accessible on the repository. This is a critical configuration error preventing any build execution. Root cause: Workflow file missing, incorrect path, or insufficient token permissions to access Actions API.

errors.title: GitHub Actions Workflow File Not Found (HTTP 404)
errors.description: The workflow dispatch API endpoint returned HTTP 404 Not Found when attempting to trigger the build.yml workflow. This indicates the workflow file does not exist at the expected path (.github/workflows/build.yml) or the GitHub token lacks necessary permissions to access the Actions API.
errors.error_message: HTTP Error 404: Not Found. Workflow dispatch endpoint returned 404. Possible causes: token missing workflow/actions permissions, workflow not accessible on this repo/branch, or Actions disabled. dispatch_url=https://api.github.com/repos/loginkarun/scib_demo/actions/workflows/.github%2Fworkflows%2Fbuild.yml/dispatches response_body={
errors.location: Repository: loginkarun/scib_demo, Branch: main, Workflow Path: .github/workflows/build.yml
errors.root_cause: The workflow file build.yml does not exist in the .github/workflows/ directory of the repository, or the GitHub personal access token lacks the 'workflow' or 'actions:write' scope required to dispatch workflows via the GitHub Actions API.
errors.impact: Complete build pipeline failure. No builds can be triggered or executed until the workflow file is created or token permissions are corrected. This blocks all CI/CD automation, testing, and deployment processes.

errors.title: GitHub Token Permission Insufficient
errors.description: The GitHub personal access token may be missing required scopes (workflow, actions:write) to interact with the GitHub Actions API for workflow dispatch operations.
errors.error_message: token missing workflow/actions permissions - API returned 404 when attempting workflow dispatch
errors.root_cause: The personal access token used for authentication does not have the necessary OAuth scopes. GitHub Actions API requires 'workflow' scope for workflow_dispatch events and 'actions:write' for triggering workflows.
errors.impact: Unable to programmatically trigger builds via API. Manual workflow triggers may still work through GitHub UI, but automated CI/CD pipelines are blocked.

errors.title: Workflow File Missing or Incorrect Path
errors.description: The build.yml workflow file is not present in the .github/workflows/ directory, or the file path reference is incorrect in the dispatch API call.
errors.error_message: Workflow file not found - Final attempt failure
errors.location: Expected Path: .github/workflows/build.yml in repository loginkarun/scib_demo on branch main
errors.root_cause: The workflow file has not been committed to the repository, was deleted, or exists under a different name/path. The dispatch API expects the exact path '.github/workflows/build.yml' to exist.
errors.impact: No build workflow definition exists to execute Maven builds, tests, or deployments. The CI/CD pipeline infrastructure is incomplete.

recommended_fixes.description: Create the missing build.yml workflow file in the .github/workflows/ directory with proper Maven build configuration.
recommended_fixes.automated_fix: Use GitHub Feature Branch Script Committer to create .github/workflows/build.yml with Maven build steps (checkout, setup-java, mvn clean install, test execution).
manual_steps: 2. Create file build.yml with workflow_dispatch trigger
3. Add Maven build job with JDK setup and build commands
4. Commit and push to main branch
5. Verify workflow appears in Actions tab
recommended_fixes.validation: After creation, verify workflow file exists by calling GitHub API: GET /repos/loginkarun/scib_demo/contents/.github/workflows/build.yml

recommended_fixes.description: Regenerate the GitHub personal access token with required scopes: 'workflow', 'actions:write', 'repo'.
recommended_fixes.automated_fix: Not automatable - requires manual token regeneration in GitHub Settings > Developer settings > Personal access tokens.
manual_steps: 1. Navigate to GitHub Settings > Developer settings > Personal access tokens
2. Generate new token (classic) or update existing token
4. Copy new token and update in automation scripts
5. Test token with appropriate API endpoint
recommended_fixes.validation: Test workflow dispatch with new token - should return HTTP 204 (success) instead of 404.

recommended_fixes.description: Ensure GitHub Actions is enabled for the repository and workflow permissions are correctly configured.
recommended_fixes.automated_fix: Check via GitHub API: GET /repos/loginkarun/scib_demo - verify 'has_actions' is true.
manual_steps: 1. Go to repository Settings > Actions > General
2. Verify 'Actions permissions' is set to 'Allow all actions and reusable workflows'
3. Check 'Workflow permissions' - set to 'Read and write permissions'
4. Ensure 'Allow GitHub Actions to create and approve pull requests' is enabled if needed
5. Save changes and retry workflow dispatch
recommended_fixes.validation: Confirm Actions tab is visible in repository and shows no disabled state.

compliance_notes.audit_trail: All workflow dispatch attempts logged with timestamps and error responses. Token usage tracked for security audit.
compliance_notes.security_impact: Token permissions should follow principle of least privilege. Current 404 error prevents unauthorized workflow execution, which is a security positive, but blocks legitimate automation.
compliance_notes.remediation_tracking: Track fix implementation: 1) Workflow file creation (ETA: immediate), 2) Token update (ETA: 1 hour), 3) Permissions verification (ETA: immediate)

action_url: N/A - Workflow dispatch failed before run creation
next_steps: 1. IMMEDIATE: Create .github/workflows/build.yml file with Maven build configuration
2. IMMEDIATE: Verify GitHub Actions is enabled in repository settings
3. HIGH PRIORITY: Regenerate GitHub token with 'workflow' and 'actions:write' scopes
4. VALIDATION: Test workflow dispatch after fixes - expect HTTP 204 response
5. MONITORING: Set up alerts for workflow dispatch failures to catch future configuration issues
